---
title: "analysis"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r}
library(ctsem)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
# Load helper functions
source("helper_funcs.R")
```


```{r}
output_dir_path <- "output/"

# Check if the directory exists
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_path)) {
  # If it doesn't exist, create it
  dir.create(output_dir_path)
  print(paste("Directory '", output_dir_path, "' created.", sep = ""))
} else {
  print(paste("Directory '", output_dir_path, "' already exists.", sep = ""))
}
```


# CHR VS. CC

## Data preparation
```{r}
# Load the data
raw_data <- read.csv("ndvi_stress_data.csv")
```

```{r}
# Data preparation
prepared_data <- data_prep(raw_data)
```


## Model Fitting

```{r}
# Whether to include Time-Dependent Predictors
include_TD <- TRUE

# Set file path based on whether TD predictors are included
if (include_TD) {
  ctsem_fit_path <- "output/fit_group_interaction_mcmc_with_TD.rds"
  # ctsem_fit_path <- "output/fit_group_interaction_MAP_with_TD.rds"
  td_predictors <- c("day", "hometime", "entropy", "locations")
} else {
  ctsem_fit_path <- "output/fit_group_interaction_mcmc.rds"
  # ctsem_fit_path <- "output/fit_group_interaction_MAP.rds"
  td_predictors <- NULL
}

if (file.exists(ctsem_fit_path)){
  cat("Loading existing fit from:", ctsem_fit_path, "\n")
  fit_interaction <- readRDS(ctsem_fit_path)
} else {
  # Specify the model
  ctsem_model_1 <- model_spec(interaction = "partial", TDpred = td_predictors)

  # fit_interaction <- ctStanFit(
  #   datalong = prepared_data,
  #   ctstanmodel = ctsem_model_1,
  #   cores = 4,
  #   optimize = TRUE, # MAP
  #   priors = TRUE,
  #   # Set the seed for reproducibility
  #   seed = 12
  # )

  fit_interaction <- ctStanFit(
    datalong = prepared_data,
    ctstanmodel = ctsem_model_1,
    cores = 4,
    optimize = FALSE,    # Use MCMC sampling instead of MAP
    iter = 2000,         # Total iterations per chain
    warmup = 1000,       # Burn-in iterations
    chains = 4,          # Number of MCMC chains
    priors = TRUE,
    control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95, max_treedepth = 12),
    seed = 12
  )

  # Save the fit object
  saveRDS(fit_interaction, file = ctsem_fit_path)
  cat("Model fitted and saved to:", ctsem_fit_path, "\n")
}
```

## Results and Interpretation
```{r}
# Parameter estimates, standard deviations (SD) and posterior credibility 
# intervals (CI) for means of estimated NDVI population distributions.
NDVI_table_1 <- create_ctsem_table_1(fit_interaction, variable = "NDVI")
print("Parameter estimates, standard deviations (SD) and posterior 
      credibility intervals (CI) for means of estimated NDVI population distributions.")
print(NDVI_table_1)
```

```{r}
# Parameter estimates, standard deviations (SD) and posterior credibility 
# intervals (CI) for means of estimated Stress population distributions.
Stress_table_1 <- create_ctsem_table_1(fit_interaction, variable = "Stress")
print("Parameter estimates, standard deviations (SD) and posterior 
      credibility intervals (CI) for means of estimated Stress population distributions.")
print(Stress_table_1)
```


```{r}
# Autoregressive and cross-lagged effects between NDVI and stress
table_2 <- create_ctsem_table_2(fit_interaction)
print(table_2)
```

```{r}
# Plot discrete-time autoregressive and cross-lagged effects of NDVI and stress
plot_discrete_effects(fit_interaction, indices = "all")
```


```{r}
# Plot discrete-time cross-lagged effects of NDVI and stress
plot_discrete_effects(fit_interaction, indices = "CR")
```

```{r}
# Calculate long-term, asymptotic correlation between the two processes in their random fluctuations
asymptotic_correlation(fit_interaction)
```

```{r}
# Time-dependent predictor effects
extract_td_effects(fit_interaction, td_predictors)
```

```{r}
# TI predictor effects of cross-lagged effects
cl_effect_moderate(fit_interaction, moderator = c("group", "sex", "age", "race", "education")) # continuous-time effect

```


## Examine group difference of cross-lagged effects
```{r}
# The interpretation should be something like A ➔ B relationship 
# was stronger in Group X than in Group Y (Est. = [], SD = [], 95 % CI [; ]).
cl_effect_moderate(fit_interaction) # continuous-time effect

# This is CHR against CC group 
```


```{r}
# Generate the Median and 95% CI of cross-lagged effects of two groups (CC and CHR) using ctStanTIpredeffects
analyze_group_effects(fit_interaction, method = 'ctStanTIpredeffects') # continuous-time effect
```


```{r}
# Generate the Median and 95% CI of cross-lagged effects of two groups (CC and CHR) using ctExtract to extract the whole posterior samples
analyze_group_effects(fit_interaction, method = 'ctExtract') # continuous-time effect
```

```{r}
# Plot discrete-time autoregressive and cross-lagged effects of NDVI and stress for each group
plot_discrete_effects_group(fit_interaction)
```

```{r}
# Plot discrete-time cross-lagged effects of NDVI and stress for each group
plot_discrete_effects_group(fit_interaction, indices = "CR")
```
